message:
test(ftui-extras): improve dot_parser.rs test coverage for DOT parsing edge cases (bd-131bo)

Add 39 new tests: graph direction (TB vs LR), edge arrow types, DotParseError
trait impls (Error, Clone, Debug), invalid input errors, nested subgraphs,
cluster member dedup, empty attrs, edge without semicolons, bare IDs with
dots/hyphens, empty quoted strings, unknown escape sequences, implicit-to-explicit
node upgrades, looks_like_dot edge cases (hash comments, case insensitive, bare
keyword), all shape mapping variants, HTML label edge cases, strip_html_tags
additional cases, undirected edge chains, large graph stability (50 nodes),
diagram type assertion, and clean parse warnings/errors.

76 total dot_parser tests pass. Clippy clean. Fmt clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-130l.4","title":"Visibility Helpers","description":"Show/hide widgets based on breakpoint.

## Requirements
- visible_at(breakpoints) filter
- hidden_at(breakpoints) filter
- Conditional rendering (not just visibility)
- Space reclamation for hidden widgets

## Implementation
- Visible wrapper widget
- hide_below, hide_above helpers
- Integration with layout solver

## Tests
- Widget visibility toggling
- Space reclamation verification
- Combined conditions

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Visibility Helpers
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-130l.6","title":"Responsive Breakpoints E2E Test Suite","description":"## Purpose
End-to-end validation of responsive layout behavior across breakpoints.

## Test Scenarios

### 1. Breakpoint Transitions (responsive_breakpoint_e2e.rs)
- Resize through all breakpoints
- Style changes at boundaries
- Layout changes at boundaries
- No flicker during transition

### 2. Style Variants (responsive_style_e2e.rs)
- Verify style at each breakpoint
- Inheritance from smaller breakpoints
- Override specificity

### 3. Layout Switching (responsive_layout_e2e.rs)
- Grid to stack at small sizes
- Column count changes
- Widget visibility changes
- State preservation across switches

### 4. Edge Cases (responsive_edge_e2e.rs)
- Rapid resize events
- Resize during animation
- Extreme aspect ratios
- Minimum viable size

## Logging Requirements
- INFO: Breakpoint changes with old/new
- DEBUG: Layout recalculation triggers
- TRACE: Style resolution chain
- Metric: Resize response latency

## Script Location
scripts/e2e/responsive/

## Snapshot Matrix
- Test at 40, 80, 120, 160 columns
- Test at 10, 24, 40 rows
- Cross-product all combinations

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Responsive Breakpoints E2E Test Suite
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

 {"id":"bd-13gzg","title":"Close GH #10: docs substantially addressed, note crates.io gap","description":"## Context
GitHub issue #10 filed by schpet (2026-02-06). Requested documentation for library consumers.

## Current State: SUBSTANTIALLY ADDRESSED
Significant documentation work was done the same day:
- docs/getting-started.md: Comprehensive guide with stability notes, crate map, prerequisites, copy-paste minimal app (~75 lines), common patterns, troubleshooting, example index
- docs/tutorials/agent-harness.md: 12 KB agent-harness tutorial
- Commit 92d4ba5d: "fix docs API drift + add CI doc/example gates"
- 5 working examples at crates/ftui-harness/examples/ (minimal, counter, layout, modal, streaming)
- ftui facade crate (crates/ftui/src/lib.rs) provides clean public API surface

## Remaining Gap
Getting-started guide recommends path dependencies (path = "../frankentui/crates/ftui") because most crates aren't on crates.io. Only ftui-core, ftui-layout, and ftui-i18n are published. The ftui facade and its transitive deps need publishing.

## Action
1. Close GH #10 with comment pointing to docs/getting-started.md and docs/tutorials/agent-harness.md
2. Note that crates.io publishing of ftui facade is the remaining gap for wider adoption
3. Note CI is also failing, which undermines confidence for library consumers

## Also note
The frankentui CI is failing (all 5 recent runs show failure). This is a separate issue but worth tracking.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-08T18:20:23.374644405Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-08T18:28:17.898516608Z","closed_at":"2026-02-08T18:28:17.898484999Z","close_reason":"Closed GH #10 with summary comment pointing to docs/getting-started.md and noting crates.io publishing gap","external_ref":"gh:Dicklesworthstone/frankentui#10","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["crates-io","docs","housekeeping"]}
 {"id":"bd-13o01","title":"demo-showcase: warm up inspector overlay stress timing","description":"Add a short warmup render before collecting timing samples in inspector overlay stress tests, reducing noise from cold caches/page faults.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-10T17:06:02.635446794Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T17:07:03.389946924Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T17:07:03.389925424Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-13pq","title":"Demo Showcase: Async Task Manager / Job Queue","description":"## Background

Modern TUIs are reactive systems: background work, progress updates, cancellations, and rate limits.

This screen demonstrates a job/task manager with:
- concurrent tasks with progress
- cancellation
- scheduling policies that are explainable and testable

The point is not "toy concurrency"; it is to prove the runtime + UI can represent and control a realistic workload.

## UX / Core Interactions

- A table/list of jobs with:
  - name
  - state (queued/running/succeeded/failed/canceled)
  - progress
  - runtime
  - priority
- Controls:
  - spawn job
  - cancel selected
  - cancel all
  - change scheduler mode

Suggested keybindings:
- N: spawn a new job
- C: cancel selected job
- S: cycle scheduler policy
- R: retry failed job

## Architecture Notes

- Determinism is mandatory for tests:
  - jobs should be driven by a deterministic clock/stepper in test mode
  - avoid wall-clock coupling in core scheduling logic

- Scheduling policies (bd-13pq.7):
  - implement as pure functions over job state + metrics
  - explicitly define objective (latency, fairness, throughput)
  - stable tie-breaking

- Cancellation (bd-13pq.8):
  - model cancellation as a state transition with audit events
  - ensure no resource leaks

## Acceptance Criteria

- Jobs progress and complete with visible updates.
- Cancellation is immediate and leaves the system consistent.
- Scheduler policies are user-visible and explainable (why this job ran next).

Additionally (project-wide standards):
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

## Alien Artifact Upgrades (Concrete)

- Queueing theory scheduler (bd-13pq.7):
  - define a small model (e.g., weighted fair scheduling vs shortest-remaining-time)
  - provide a comparison harness with deterministic traces
- Hazard-based cancellation (bd-13pq.8):
  - treat "should we cancel?" as expected-loss minimization with explicit costs
  - document assumptions and emit an evidence ledger entry when a policy triggers

## Extreme Optimization

- Budget the per-tick update overhead.
- Provide a stress test with many jobs and verify the UI remains responsive.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-02T22:31:12.257537916Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-03T17:47:53.338737861Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T17:47:53.338713265Z","close_reason":"Full implementation complete: 6 scheduling policies with queueing theory foundations, hazard-based cancellation, aging fairness, diagnostic logging with JSONL, 91 unit tests passing, 17 snapshot tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["demo","ux"],"comments":[{"id":295,"issue_id":"bd-13pq","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scope
- Spawn multiple concurrent jobs with progress + cancel.
- Show task states and a summary dashboard.

Testing
- Unit tests for task state transitions and cancellation.
- PTY E2E tests with verbose logs for job lifecycle.

+{"id":"bd-2rd1g","title":"bug(ftui-extras): DoomFireFx resize must reset buffer even when len unchanged","description":"DoomFireFx::ensure_buffer() clears heat only when old_len != new_len. If width/height change but total cell count stays the same (e.g. 12x8 -> 8x12), the heat buffer is reinterpreted with a new row stride without being cleared, leaving stale heat in non-bottom rows.

Fix: when dimensions change, clear heat[0..len] regardless of whether len changed; then re-seed bottom row when active.

Add a regression test that warms up at one size, resizes to a different (width,height) with same len, and asserts all non-bottom cells are reset to 0 (bottom row stays hot when active).","status":"in_progress","priority":3,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"RubyMill","estimated_minutes":20,"created_at":"2026-02-10T20:40:49.377428517Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T20:40:49.377428517Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"bd-2rs","title":"Implement clipboard integration (OSC 52)","description":"PHASE 7 EXTRAS - CLIPBOARD INTEGRATION

## Background
OSC 52 allows setting the system clipboard from terminal applications. This enables copy/paste functionality without external dependencies.

## Technical Requirements

### OSC 52 Protocol
Sequence: ESC ] 52 ; c ; <base64-data> BEL

c = clipboard selection:
- c = clipboard
- p = primary (X11)
- s = secondary
- 0-7 = cut buffers

### Clipboard API

pub struct Clipboard {
    caps: TerminalCapabilities,
}

impl Clipboard {
    /// Check if OSC 52 is supported
    pub fn is_available(&self) -> bool {
        self.caps.osc52
    }
    
    /// Set clipboard content
    pub fn set(&self, content: &str, writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
        if !self.is_available() {
            return Err(io::Error::new(
                io::ErrorKind::Unsupported,
                "OSC 52 not available"
            ));
        }
        
        let encoded = base64::encode(content);
        write!(writer, "\x1b]52;c;{}\x07", encoded)?;
        writer.flush()
    }
    
    /// Clear clipboard
    pub fn clear(&self, writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
        write!(writer, "\x1b]52;c;\x07")?;
        writer.flush()
    }
}

### Terminal Compatibility
OSC 52 support varies:
- ✅ iTerm2, kitty, alacritty, WezTerm, foot
- ⚠️ tmux (needs set-clipboard on)
- ⚠️ screen (needs clipboard support)
- ❌ Many terminal emulators disable by default

### Detection Strategy
1. Check known terminal from TERM/TERM_PROGRAM
2. Check tmux/screen allow-passthrough
3. Attempt set and check for response (risky)
4. Default: assume unsupported

### Size Limits
Most terminals limit OSC 52 payload:
- Common limit: 74994 bytes (base64)
- Split large content or fail gracefully

### Fallback Options
When OSC 52 unavailable:
- Fall back to external clipboard tool (feature: clipboard-fallback)
  - macOS: pbcopy/pbpaste
  - Linux: xclip, xsel, wl-copy
  - Windows: clip.exe
- Or return error for application to handle

## Tests Required
- [ ] OSC 52 sequence generation
- [ ] Base64 encoding correct
- [ ] Size limit handling
- [ ] Clear clipboard works
- [ ] Capability detection
- [ ] Fallback to external tools
- [ ] Error handling when unavailable

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] OSC 52 set clipboard
- [ ] Capability detection
- [ ] Size limit enforcement
- [ ] Clear clipboard
- [ ] Optional external fallback
- [ ] Feature gate: clipboard

## Location: ftui-extras crate (feature = "clipboard")","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:39:12.769788961Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T17:52:42.242840026Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T17:52:42.242822323Z","close_reason":"Added OSC 52 Clipboard API with feature gating and tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2rs","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:17Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3frfo","title":"Fix transaction commit-after-rollback leaking rolled-back commands","description":"In ftui-runtime undo transactions, Transaction::rollback finalizes but leaves batch intact, and Transaction::commit currently ignores finalized state and may still return Some(batch). This can push already-rolled-back commands into history (e.g., scope commit after execute failure). Fix commit semantics and add regression tests for commit-after-rollback and scope failure path.","notes":"Found during random deep-flow audit of undo transaction semantics.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"NavyDesert","created_at":"2026-02-10T01:06:45.727834453Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-10T01:47:22.214104817Z","closed_at":"2026-02-10T01:47:22.214086142Z","close_reason":"Merged in 0d433ae9; adds finalized guard + regression tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-3ftty","title":"VT conformance: JSON fixture test runner for scroll region tests","description":"Build a test runner in differential_terminal.rs (or a new test file) that loads JSON fixtures from tests/fixtures/vt-conformance/scroll_region/ and runs them through both frankenterm-core and VirtualTerminal. The 26 existing JSON fixtures define input_bytes_hex and expected cursor/cell state. The runner should: (1) deserialize fixture JSON, (2) decode hex bytes, (3) feed through both engines, (4) compare actual vs expected state, (5) compare both engines against each other. This complements bd-3d7xh and bd-1ivrz.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"PurpleHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-09T13:08:07.599830902Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T13:18:02.912282154Z","closed_at":"2026-02-09T13:18:02.912263029Z","close_reason":"JSON fixture test runner implemented and passing: 26 fixtures loaded, 25 pass (1 tracked known failure: DECOM support).  {"id":"bd-3fu8","title":"Semantic Events: Core Types and SemanticEvent Enum","description":"## Task

Define the core `SemanticEvent` enum and related types in `ftui-core`.

## Implementation

Create `crates/ftui-core/src/semantic_event.rs`:

```rust
use crate::{KeyCode, Modifiers, MouseButton, Position};
use std::time::Duration;

/// High-level semantic events derived from raw terminal input.
/// These represent user intentions rather than raw input.
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq)]
pub enum SemanticEvent {
    // === Mouse Gestures ===
    
    /// Single click (mouse down + up in same position)
    Click {
        pos: Position,
        button: MouseButton,
    },
    
    /// Two clicks within threshold time
    DoubleClick {
        pos: Position,
        button: MouseButton,
    },
    
    /// Three clicks within threshold (often used for line selection)
    TripleClick {
        pos: Position,
        button: MouseButton,
    },
    
    /// Mouse held down beyond threshold without moving
    LongPress {
        pos: Position,
        duration: Duration,
    },
    
    // === Drag Gestures ===
    
    /// Mouse moved beyond drag threshold while button held
    DragStart {
        pos: Position,
        button: MouseButton,
    },
    
    /// Ongoing drag movement
    DragMove {
        start: Position,
        current: Position,
        delta: (i16, i16),  // Movement since last DragMove
    },
    
    /// Mouse released after drag
    DragEnd {
        start: Position,
        end: Position,
    },
    
    /// Drag cancelled (Escape pressed, focus lost, etc.)
    DragCancel,
    
    // === Keyboard Gestures ===
    
    /// Key chord sequence completed (e.g., Ctrl+K Ctrl+C)
    Chord {
        sequence: Vec<ChordKey>,
    },
    
    // === Touch-Like Gestures ===
    
    /// Swipe gesture (rapid mouse movement)
    Swipe {
        direction: SwipeDirection,
        distance: u16,
        velocity: f32,  // cells per second
    },
}

/// A key in a chord sequence
#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq, Hash)]
pub struct ChordKey {
    pub code: KeyCode,
    pub modifiers: Modifiers,
}

/// Direction for swipe gestures
#[derive(Debug, Clone, Copy, PartialEq, Eq)]
pub enum SwipeDirection {
    Up,
    Down,
    Left,
    Right,
}

impl SemanticEvent {
    /// Returns true if this is a drag-related event
    pub fn is_drag(&self) -> bool {
        matches\!(self, Self::DragStart { .. } | Self::DragMove { .. } | Self::DragEnd { .. } | Self::DragCancel)
    }
    
    /// Returns the position if this event has one
    pub fn position(&self) -> Option<Position> {
        match self {
            Self::Click { pos, .. } |
            Self::DoubleClick { pos, .. } |
            Self::TripleClick { pos, .. } |
            Self::LongPress { pos, .. } |
            Self::DragStart { pos, .. } => Some(*pos),
            Self::DragMove { current, .. } => Some(*current),
            Self::DragEnd { end, .. } => Some(*end),
            _ => None,
        }
    }
}
```

## Unit Tests

```rust
#[cfg(test)]
mod tests {
    use super::*;

    #[test]
    fn semantic_event_is_drag() {
        assert\!(SemanticEvent::DragStart { 
            pos: Position { x: 0, y: 0 }, 
            button: MouseButton::Left 
        }.is_drag());
        
        assert\!(\!SemanticEvent::Click { 
            pos: Position { x: 0, y: 0 }, 
            button: MouseButton::Left 
        }.is_drag());
    }

    #[test]
    fn semantic_event_position() {
        let click = SemanticEvent::Click { 
            pos: Position { x: 5, y: 10 }, 
            button: MouseButton::Left 
        };
        assert_eq\!(click.position(), Some(Position { x: 5, y: 10 }));
        
        let chord = SemanticEvent::Chord { sequence: vec\![] };
        assert_eq\!(chord.position(), None);
    }

    #[test]
    fn chord_key_equality() {
        let k1 = ChordKey { code: KeyCode::Char('k'), modifiers: Modifiers::CONTROL };
        let k2 = ChordKey { code: KeyCode::Char('k'), modifiers: Modifiers::CONTROL };
        let k3 = ChordKey { code: KeyCode::Char('c'), modifiers: Modifiers::CONTROL };
        
        assert_eq\!(k1, k2);
        assert_ne\!(k1, k3);
    }
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] SemanticEvent enum with all variants
- [ ] ChordKey and SwipeDirection types
- [ ] Helper methods (is_drag, position)
- [ ] Unit tests passing
- [ ] Exported from ftui-core lib.rs
- [ ] Documentation with examples

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## E2E Plan
- Provide/extend an E2E script that exercises the feature end-to-end in PTY (where applicable).
- Logging: verbose JSONL with env/capabilities + per-step timings + seed + checksums; include a summary at end.
- On failure: print reproduction command and point to artifacts directory.
